title: Tech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Amazon Fire TV’s new interface is now rolling out in the U.S.
items.description: Fire TV's new interface simplifies its layout and navigation and adds Alexa+.

items.title: Apple is reportedly cooking up a trio of AI wearables
items.description: As the AI hardware space heats up, the iPhone maker has multiple smart products in development.

items.title: Apple Podcasts is getting an enhanced video experience this spring
items.description: By bringing an enhanced video podcasts viewing experience to its app, Apple is aiming to keep users from turning to rival platforms to watch their fav...

items.title: India bids to attract over $200B in AI infrastructure investment by 2028
items.description: India is ramping up shared AI compute, adding 20,000 GPUs as part of a broader push to attract global AI investment.

items.title: Here are the 17 US-based AI companies that have raised $100m or more in 2026
items.description: Three U.S.-based AI companies raised rounds larger than $1 billion so far in 2026 with 14 others raising rounds of $100 million or more.

items.title: Anthropic releases Sonnet 4.6
items.description: Anthropic has released a new version of its mid-size Sonnet model, keeping pace with the company's four-month update cycle.

items.title: Thrive raises $10B for new fund, its largest yet
items.description: Thrive Capital just raised $10 billion for its new fund-- nearly double the size of its last fund.

items.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
items.description: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, inclu...

items.title: Climactic launches hybrid fund to get startups through the ‘valley of death’
items.description: The new project, called Material Scale, will initially focus on climate tech startups in the apparel industry.

items.title: Meta’s own research found parental supervision doesn’t really help curb teens’ compulsive social media use
items.description: An internal research study at Meta found that parental supervision may not help teens regulate their social media, and teens with trauma are more incl...
